ocr.text: The table presents a comprehensive evaluation of various studies with respect to their methodological rigor and bias potential.
The study by Wang 2014 [6] exhibits study participation as Yes, study attrition as Yes, prognostic factor measurement as Yes,
outcome measurement as Partly, confounding measurement and account as Yes, analysis approaches as Yes, and an overall
appraisal of potential bias as Moderate.
Study  Study partici-pation  Study attrition  Prognostic factor measurement  Outcome measure-ment  Confounding measurement and account  Analysis approaches  Overall appraisal of potential bias
Wang 2014 [6]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Partly  Yes  Yes  Moderate
Siegel 2014 [7]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Low
Cubukcu 2014 [10]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Low
Canhoroz 2014 [24]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Low
Lee 2014 [9]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Low
Shin 2014 [8]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Low
Kerenidi 2013 [11]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Partly  No  Yes  High
Tsukada 2011 [12]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Partly  Yes  Yes  Moderate
Petridou 2009 [13]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  Low
Ferroni 2007 [25]  Yes  Yes  Yes  Yes  No  Yes  High
Siegel 2014 [7] shows all parameters as Yes, leading to a low appraisal of potential bias. Similarly, Cubukcu 2014 [10],
Canhoroz 2014 [24], Lee 2014 [9], and Shin 2014 [8] all maintain study participation, study attrition, prognostic factor
measurement, outcome measurement, confounding measurement, and analysis approaches as Yes, resulting in a low bias
appraisal for each. In contrast, Kerenidi 2013 [11] has study participation, study attrition, and prognostic factor measurement as
Yes, but shows outcome measurement as Partly, confounding measurement and account as No, and analysis approaches as Yes,
culminating in a high bias appraisal. Tsukada 2011 [12] shares a similar pattern with a moderate bias appraisal where study
participation, study attrition, and prognostic factor measurement are Yes, outcome measurement is Partly, confounding
measurement is Yes, and analysis approaches are Yes. Lastly, Petridou 2009 [13] has all parameters as Yes, leading to a low bias
appraisal, while Ferroni 2007 [25] has Yes for study participation, study attrition, prognostic factor measurement, and outcome
measurement, but No for confounding measurement and account, with analysis approaches as Yes, resulting in a high appraisal
of potential bias.
